summary.intent: Review Greptile's automated code review comments on PR #432 in moltis-org/moltis, fix the flagged issues, and resolve the review conversations.
summary.outcome: Fixed two issues flagged in a new Greptile review (reverted dead-code addition to `close_client`, removed an unrelated migration file), committed the changes, and replied to resolve the review threads. A prior Greptile review had already been addressed in the codebase before the session began.

repo: The moltis repo uses worktrees at /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/<branch-name>/
`close_client` in crates/gateway/src/state.rs is dead code that pre-existed on main — it has no callers anywhere in the codebase
node_count is managed via atomic store/fetch_sub in state.rs; disconnect_all_clients resets it to 0 at line 876

code.finding: disconnect_all_clients resets node_count to 0 with Ordering::Relaxed after clearing nodes — this was the fix for the first Greptile issue
GatewayNodeExecProvider::new() takes both state and node_count parameters — the disconnected-counter footgun constructor was already removed before the session
close_client had was_node/fetch_sub logic added by this PR, but since close_client is never called, that logic was dead code and was reverted

workflow: Greptile may post multiple sequential reviews on a PR; always fetch the latest review, not just the first one
When Greptile flags dead code, check git history to determine if it was dead before the PR or introduced by it — the fix differs in each case
Replying to review threads via API and then resolving them are separate steps; threads can already be resolved before you attempt to resolve them

friction: User had to ask twice for the PR review fix before a newer Greptile review was noticed — the first pass only checked the already-resolved older review
Resolving GitHub review threads requires multiple API calls (reply + resolve) and the thread was already resolved when attempted, suggesting state wasn't checked before acting
open_items: `close_client` remains dead code on main — it was not deleted, only the PR-introduced changes were reverted; the dead function itself should be cleaned up separately
The unrelated migration crates/sessions/migrations/20260306160000_session_tool_mode.sql was removed from this PR branch but presumably still needs to land somewhere
